mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit growth (RSAFS) expands while industrial production (INDPRO) stagnates, it indicates demand outstripping supply capacity, fueling inflation expectations that compress equity multiples and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, higher short-term rates initially compress equity valuations, but once the federal funds rate peaks and stabilizes, the market prices in a soft landing, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain negative or flat for 60 days following the identified rate peak, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for contemporaneous CPIAUCSL momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) reflect improved consumer confidence and forward-looking economic activity, which directly supports corporate revenue expectations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between HOUST month-over-month changes and 5-day forward SPX returns is consistently below 0.05 across all rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index below 105 signals manufacturing contraction, reducing corporate profit margins and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO crosses below 105, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) grow robustly while real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates, it indicates labor market lagging behind overall economic momentum, a classic late-cycle divergence that precedes equity drawdowns and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where consumer credit (RSAFS) accelerates while CPIAUCSL remains sticky above 3.0%, equity markets experience a final risk-on rally driven by liquidity before inflation fears trigger a correction, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative within 5 days when RSAFS growth exceeds its 12-month moving average, or the signal's hit rate falls below 50% in out-of-sample testing.
